headlines.title: Oil prices jump 5% after U.S. revokes Iran oil sanctions waiver following ship attacks
headlines.description: Oil prices jumped 5% after attacks on tankers in the Strait of Hormuz prompted the Trump administration to revoke a sanctions waiver on sales of Iranian oil.

headlines.title: Anthropic will make Claude Cowork available to users via the cloud
headlines.description: The leading AI company announced its move from a laptop-based system to one that allows users to use Cowork throughout the day and across devices.

headlines.title: Gas cost calculator: How much have higher gas prices cost you?
headlines.description: Americans have spent an estimated $33 billion more in gas since the Iran war started. According to an NBC News analysis, drivers who fill up twice a month paid anywhere from $20 to $300 more for gas.

headlines.title: Walmart Announces Sweeping Price Cuts on Thousands of Items
headlines.description: Despite rising inflation, Walmart announced sweeping price cuts on thousands of everyday essentials. The retail giant said you’ll see savings in groceries, including backyard cook-out items like ground beef, corn, ice cream and soda. It’s also lowering prices for household essentials, outdoor living items, toys and apparel.

headlines.title: How to Test if Being a Stay-at-Home Parent is Right For You
headlines.description: Finance expert Farnoosh Torabi, host of the “So Money” podcast, joins TODAY’s Jenna Bush Hager and Sheinelle Jones to help viewers tackle financial dilemmas and share some smart money advice. Viewer questions include how to manage being a stay-at-home parent and more.

headlines.title: How to Talk to Your Kids About Finances and Money Management
headlines.description: A recent survey found that over 60% of parents plant to start teaching their kids about money before the age of 12. Yanely Espinal, director of educational outreach at the nonprofit Next Gen Personal Finance and author of “Mind Your Money,” joins TODAY to share tools to help spark conversations with children about money habits.

headlines.title: U.S. economy adds 57,000 jobs in June as the unemployment rate dips to 4.2%
headlines.description: The U.S. economy added 57,000 jobs in June, a worrying sign for labor market stability as wage growth tracked below inflation for a third consecutive month. NBC News' Allie Canal and Investopedia editor-in-chief Caleb Silver break down the latest numbers.

headlines.title: How grocery prices have changed since Trump took office
headlines.description: Learn where grocery prices stand during the Trump administration. See how inflation is affecting the cost of eggs, bread, chicken, ground beef and more.

headlines.title: Trump’s ‘big, beautiful bill’ is bringing a big set of student loan changes
headlines.description: Starting Wednesday, many student borrowers may tackle higher repayment requirements, fewer program options and greater loan limits.

headlines.title: New York City-bound flight from Kenya diverted over malfunctioning flight controls
headlines.description: The Kenya Airways flight turned back to its departure airport, Jomo Kenyatta International in the capital city, Nairobi, with no other issues reported.

headlines.title: Firework strikes Delta flight landing at Chicago’s Midway Airport
headlines.description: The aircraft landed safely and taxied to the gate, the airline said, adding that the incident is under review.

headlines.title: Track U.S., state and county gas prices
headlines.description: Are gas prices declining? NBC News is tracking national, state and county prices at the pump.
